clusters.topic: Most Regretted College Majors 2026: Poli Sci at 46%, Nursing at 0%
clusters.summary: ZipRecruiter survey of 3,000 graduates shows poli sci tops regret at 46.3%, comms at 39.2%, physical sciences at 33.3%. 1 in 5 grads regret. Nursing least regretted ($70K starting). Health admin grads face 43.8% salary shortfall.
clusters.student_reaction_summary: Major choice is a universal anxiety. Ranked lists always drive comments.
clusters.factual_basis_summary: CBS News and AcademicJobs reporting on ZipRecruiter survey of 3,000 Class of 2025/2026 graduates.
clusters.possible_content_angle: Most vs least regretted majors ranked with salary reality gap

clusters.topic: Workforce Pell Grants Launch July 1: Free Money for 8-Week Certificates
clusters.summary: Starting July 1, Pell Grants can fund short-term programs (8-15 weeks, 150-599 hours) in IT, healthcare, trades, early childhood ed. Must have 70% completion and 70% job placement rates. Previously required 600+ hours over 15+ weeks.
clusters.student_reaction_summary: Game-changer for students considering alternatives to 4-year degrees. High decision-usefulness.
clusters.factual_basis_summary: Official ED press release and final rule published May 19, 2026. Confirmed by EdSource and industry outlets.
clusters.possible_content_angle: Pell Grants now cover 8-week certificates: which programs qualify and how to apply

clusters.topic: College Meal Plans: 47% Price Jump in 10 Years, and 98% of Schools Force You to Buy One
clusters.summary: Meal plan prices jumped 47% in 10 years vs 26% general food inflation. 98% of schools require freshmen to buy. Average lowest tier is $5,656. Wellesley charges $7,442/year — 3x home cooking cost. 40-50% more than USDA estimates.
clusters.student_reaction_summary: Universal frustration — every freshman deals with this. Texas Tech student newspaper editorial shows active student pushback.
clusters.factual_basis_summary: Hechinger Report investigative journalism with USDA comparison data. Money.com with specific cost figures. Student newspaper editorial adds campus voice.
clusters.possible_content_angle: The hidden fee you can't avoid: what your meal plan ACTUALLY costs vs cooking yourself

clusters.topic: 80% of CC Students Want a Bachelor's — Only 16% Actually Get One
clusters.summary: CCRC/Columbia research: only 16% of CC students earn bachelor's within 6 years (up from 14% in 2016). 80% intend to transfer. Low-income at 11%, Black students at 9%. Credit loss, unclear articulation agreements, financial disruption are barriers.
clusters.student_reaction_summary: Devastating gap between intention and outcome. High relevance for CC students and those considering the CC-to-university pathway.
clusters.factual_basis_summary: CCRC at Columbia University tracking study. Higher Ed Dive reporting on peer-reviewed data.
clusters.possible_content_angle: The 80% vs 16% gap: why most CC students never finish the transfer, and how to beat the odds

clusters.topic: Skills-Based Hiring: 70% of Employers Dropped Degree Requirements
clusters.summary: 70%+ of employers now use skills-based hiring. Only 28% of tech job descriptions require a degree. Google, IBM, Tesla dropped requirements. Federal government overhauled hiring April 2026. But degree holders still earn $30K+ more annually.
clusters.student_reaction_summary: Fuels 'is college worth it' debate. Non-STEM students anxious about ROI.
clusters.factual_basis_summary: Multiple employer surveys. FedSmith on federal hiring overhaul. Enrollment data from NSCRC.
clusters.possible_content_angle: Skills checklist by industry: what employers actually want if not your degree

clusters.topic: Enrollment Cliff Starts Fall 2026: What Happens to Your Campus
clusters.summary: 15.5M undergrads in Spring 2026 — historic peak. High school grads drop from 3.9M (2025) to 3.4M by 2041. Certificates surged 10.2% while master's fell 1.3%. Small privates most at risk.
clusters.student_reaction_summary: Abstract but implications are real: program cuts, tuition hikes, campus closures.
clusters.factual_basis_summary: NSCRC data, WICHE projections — peer-reviewed demographic data.
clusters.possible_content_angle: Which types of schools are most at risk and what it means for current students

clusters.topic: Greek Life: Booming at SEC Schools, Dying at Small Colleges
clusters.summary: NPC reports 13% increase in sorority recruitment. But Knox College saw 50% drop in 3 years. Gen Z drinks less, values authenticity, and is more diverse. Enrollment cliff threatens Greek housing at smaller schools in Northeast/Midwest.
clusters.student_reaction_summary: Polarizing topic — students either love or hate Greek life. Regional split adds comparison angle.
clusters.factual_basis_summary: Yahoo/The Conversation reporting. AFA industry data. Knox Student newspaper with specific institutional data.
clusters.possible_content_angle: Is Greek life dying? Depends on your school — SEC vs small college split

clusters.topic: Gen Z Searching for Scholarships on TikTok Instead of FAFSA
clusters.summary: CNBC and Inside Higher Ed report Gen Z students turning to TikTok for scholarship advice instead of official channels. Behavioral shift in how students find financial aid.
clusters.student_reaction_summary: Interesting behavior trend but less actionable than other topics.
clusters.factual_basis_summary: CNBC April 2026, Inside Higher Ed April 2026 — both strong outlets.
clusters.possible_content_angle: TikTok vs FAFSA: where Gen Z looks for money and what they're missing

clusters.topic: 37.6% of College Students Are Behind on Credit Card Payments
clusters.summary: Average student credit card debt $3,280. 37.6% behind on payments. 44.7% pay only minimum. 57% claim 'emergencies only' but still accumulate debt. 70.1% use for online shopping.
clusters.student_reaction_summary: 'Emergency only' paradox will resonate — students recognize themselves in this data.
clusters.factual_basis_summary: WalletHub and CardRates data aggregation from credit bureau and survey data.
clusters.possible_content_angle: The 'emergency only' lie: 57% say that but average debt is $3,280 — credit card reality check

clusters.topic: 66.6% of Students Skip Buying Textbooks Because of Cost
clusters.summary: Average $1,298/year for textbooks. 66.6% decided not to buy because of cost. OER initiatives saving millions at some schools. Legislative push at state level for affordability.
clusters.student_reaction_summary: Universal experience — every student has skipped buying a textbook. Practical tips angle.
clusters.factual_basis_summary: Research.com citing College Board data. MSU Denver RED on legislative angle.
clusters.possible_content_angle: 2 out of 3 students skip buying textbooks: how to get everything you need for free (legally)

excluded_items.reason: Student loan topics covered 6/19 (#3) and 6/22 (#5) — 3-day cooldown
AI academic integrity covered 6/19 (#2) and 6/22 (#2) — 5-day cooldown
Entry-level job market / salary covered 6/19 (#1) — overlap
